info.title: Human Service API
info.description: Scrapes and caches a person's online presence (websites, LinkedIn, blog, etc.) to provide writing context for AI content generation.

provider.description: Lead provider to route to. Explicit choice wins over `need`.

total.description: Total matchable. apify: pipelinelabs-only signal (provider cursor, not a cross-source-exact total).

nextOffset.description: apify offset for the next page (null when done / apollo cursor-based).

provider.description: Lead provider to route to. Explicit choice wins over `need`.

providerPersonId.description: apollo person id (usable for a later enrich). null for apify.

provider.description: Lead provider to route to. Explicit choice wins over `need`.

need.description: Intent routing: 'verified_email' routes to apify. Ignored if `provider` is set.

nextPage.description: apollo only: omit filters and advance the server-managed cursor for the next page.

limit.description: apify only: max leads to return (provider cap 1000). Defaults to 1 — apify bills per returned lead (each hit carries a verified email; no free teaser list), so the gateway takes the strict minimum unless you consciously raise it to batch.

offset.description: apify only: pagination offset (pass back `nextOffset` from the prior page).

audienceId.description: Tag returned (served) leads as members of this audience (provenance membership). The audience must belong to the org (404 otherwise). Tagging applies to billed serves only — apify search hits (every hit is billed). Does not change which filters are searched; the caller supplies filters as usual.

functions.description: Job functions. Honored by apify only — apollo has no functions search filter.

companyNames.description: Company names. Honored by apify only — apollo searches by domain/industry, not name.

technologies.description: Tech stack. apify + apollo (apollo technology UIDs).

provider.description: Lead provider to route to. Explicit choice wins over `need`.
Defaults to apollo (same as search). Set 'apify' for the verified-email waterfall. The reveal follows the provider that searched — a provider person id only means something to its own provider.

providerPersonId.description: apollo only: the apollo person id returned by a prior search. Reveals the verified email via apollo /enrich (the billed path, 1 credit). PREFERRED for apollo — apollo search masks last name + domain, so identity-based match can't be satisfied from a search hit.

includeInferred.description: apify only: include pattern-inferred emails in the waterfall.

audienceId.description: Tag the resolved (served) person as a member of this audience (provenance membership). The audience must belong to the org (404 otherwise).

provider.description: Lead provider to route to. Explicit choice wins over `need`.
Lead provider to route to. Explicit choice wins over `need`.
Lead provider to route to. Explicit choice wins over `need`.

count.description: Live match count for these filters on this provider (free dry-run). 0 means the LLM could not find a non-empty, valid filter set within the retry budget.

validationError.description: Set when the provider rejected the filters as invalid after the retry budget was exhausted.

truncated.description: True when the NL implied more segments than the per-provider cap and the LLM grouped coarser.

nlPrompt.description: Natural-language audience description. The LLM reads the caller's own granularity intent from this text (e.g. 'split by country', 'founders in FR and DE separately') and emits one candidate per implied segment — granularity and count are NOT separate inputs.

provider.description: The provider this audience commits to, when persisting a candidate from /suggest. Omit for a neutral audience.

apolloCount.description: Optional count snapshot the caller already obtained from /orgs/people/search/dry-run (apollo). Stored as-is; refresh-count re-computes it server-side later.

inserted.description: Personas written as new audiences (0 on a dry-run or a re-run where all already exist).

skipped.description: Personas whose id already exists as an audience (idempotent no-op).
